items.title: Perth man wants compensation for forensic examination damage

children.content: Troy Coward's investment property was the scene of a brutal murder involving his tenants. More than 13 years later, he is fighting for compensation from police over the impact he says their investigation had on his health and finances.

gallery.altText: Man with short hair and dark brown blazer, arms folded, with trees and pathway in background
Troy Coward's East Perth apartment bathroom covered in smudgy black marks
The laundry area of Troy Coward's former apartment

Troy Coward's East Perth apartment bathroom covered in smudgy black marks

items.title: Ignoring One Nation like 'ignoring will of people', Basil Zempilas warns

children.content: WA Opposition Leader Basil Zempilas says One Nation deserves "respect" and he is open to finding a way of working with the right-wing populist party as support for it grows.

image.altText: Basil Zempilas stands at a podium and addresses the crowd

items.title: Obscene acts charges prompt calls for more CCTV

children.content: A woman who reported a terrifying incident while walking at night in Derby says more CCTV cameras would increase safety and help monitor popular locations.

image.altText: The main strip of Derby, a narrow road surrounded by red dirt and bushy trees.

children.content: Birdwatchers from across Perth and even interstate have flocked to WA's Midwest after the sighting of a black-headed gull.

gallery.altText: a silver grey gull with black head wings spread in flight over a beach

Man crawls commando style on beach with large camera and gulls

items.title: WA opposition set to introduce stronger train lighting legislation

children.content: Advocates welcome a WA opposition bill enforcing better train lighting, after decades of campaigning.

image.altText: Three women holding bouquets of flowers walk to a place near a paddock to put the flowers.

items.title: Mechelle Turvey among 'outstanding' Australians on King's Birthday Honours roll

children.content: Almost 950 Australians are honoured, including five stand-out recipients who are now Companions of the Order — among them, the mother of murdered Noongar-Yamatji schoolboy Cassius Turvey.

image.altText: A graphic with the images of three women and two men.

items.title: Daniel Turpin named as diver killed in Albany shark attack

children.content: The family of a 35-year-old diver who died after being bitten by a shark has described him as "an adored husband, son, brother and uncle who had a lifelong love and deep respect for the ocean".

articles.title: Diver dies after being bitten by 4.5m shark off Albany

children.content: A male diver has died after an attack by a suspected 4.5-metre shark near Michaelmas Island, off WA's Albany coast, on Saturday morning.

gallery.altText: A man with short brown hair and a white shirt.

thumbnail.altText: A man with short brown hair and a white shirt.

items.title: Could this famous architect-turned-priest be named WA's first saint?

children.content: The life of a world famous architect-turned-Catholic priest is being examined to decide if he should be named Western Australia's first saint. John Cyril Hawes constructed more than a dozen famous church buildings across regional WA.

gallery.altText: Image of John Hawes sitting in a collard coat and looking at the camera. Beside him is a small dog.
Monsignor John Hawes and a worker sit on a platform
A man and his dog in statue sit on a round fountain in front of a cathedral. Bird fly in the sky.

Monsignor John Hawes and a worker sit on a platform
A man and his dog in statue sit on a round fountain in front of a cathedral. Bird fly in the sky.

items.title: Trump storms out of interview after grilling on 'crooked' election claims

children.content: US President Donald Trump angrily storms out of an interview with American broadcaster NBC after he was questioned about his unfounded claims of election fraud.

image.altText: A man in a navy suit and red tie, sitting for an interview

items.title: Private medical imaging provider bills Medicare for non-eligible services

children.content: A private medical imaging provider operating in a public hospital has been billing Medicare for some services that are not eligible for bulk billing, according to an internal memo obtained by the ABC.

items.title: Meet the man born, raised and married in his local town hall

children.content: Brian Baker, 95, spent the first 21 years of his life in living quarters at the York Town Hall where his father was caretaker.

gallery.altText: Man on mobility scooter dwarfed by large ornate stage and red velvet curtains
A man and woman in ballroom dancing stance smile at the camera
An elderly man looks at a black and white photo of a young woman

items.title: Anthropic has its eyes on Australia — and a large slice of its electricity output

children.content: The world's biggest tech titans see Australia as a promising investment opportunity for their AI ambitions, but some fear the federal government's regulatory retreat could lead to harm.

image.altText: A person holding an iPhone with Character.aI, ChatGPT, Claude and Grok chatbot apps on screen.

items.title: Top neurologists say regulator ignored warnings as weed killer decision looms

children.content: Australia's leading movement disorder specialists say the chemical regulator has not engaged with their concerns about paraquat, despite growing evidence linking the herbicide to Parkinson's disease.

image.altText: A man wearing dark jumper and beige puffer coat speaks, seen from behind the shoulder of someone wearing purple

items.title: History quiz: When was the first Mars Exploration Rover launch?

children.content: What make of car was stolen in the world's first official vehicle theft? And do you know where the Endeavour was repaired after hitting the Great Barrier Reef in 1770?

image.altText: A black and white image of a robot arm surveying the rocky, barren surface of Mars.

items.title: Aussie pole vaulter hands Duplantis first loss in more than 1,000 days

children.content: Kurtis Marschall pulls off a major upset, defeating world record holder Armand "Mondo" Duplantis at the Stockholm Diamond League meet.

image.altText: Kurtis Marschall celebrates winning the men's pole vault at the Stockholm Diamond League meet.

items.title: The electric classic car that now drives like a 'pocket rocket'

children.content: It may look like a small vintage car, but an EV conversion has brought it "up to speed" to its modern counterparts.

items.title: Piastri promoted after penalty as teen sensation wins wild Monaco GP

children.content: Teenage sensation Kimi Antonelli was flawless to lead every lap of a drama-filled Monaco Grand Prix, claiming a fifth consecutive win to extend his lead in the Formula 1 drivers' championship. Follow live.

image.altText: Oscar Piastri at the Monaco Grand Prix 2026

items.title: Marilyn's world 'fell apart' with one post. What happened next helped her heal

children.content: Marilyn was just five when she first encountered grief. Now 18, the teenager says she's been to more funerals than birthday parties.

image.altText: A young woman with a flower tucked behind her ear smiles as she holds onto her necklace.

items.title: Farmers question plan to build youth centre near Ord cotton properties

children.content: The decision to locate Kununurra's new youth centre on an abandoned farm 7km from town has sparked local concerns.
